topic: reflecting on the year — grateful for job change and engagement

turns.content: I've been in a really reflective mood lately and I just want to talk about it if that's ok
Of course, I'm here. What's on your mind?

so I was looking at my photos from this time last year and it's kind of wild how much has changed. like a year ago I was stressed out of my mind, unhappy at work, uncertain about everything and now things are just... better? not perfect but genuinely better

That's a really meaningful thing to recognize. Sometimes when you're in the middle of change you can't see the progress, but looking back a full year gives you real perspective. What feels like the biggest shift for you?

honestly the job change was everything. I didn't realize how much my old situation was draining me until I got out of it. like I was going to work every day with this pit in my stomach and I thought that was just what work felt like but it turns out no, it was that specific environment

That's such a common realization — you normalize the stress until you're in a better environment and suddenly realize how heavy it was. It sounds like the change gave you back energy you didn't even know you were missing. Are you feeling more settled now?

yeah so much more settled. like I actually look forward to going in now which sounds weird for someone who works nights but the team is great and I feel respected and the work is challenging in a good way not in a soul crushing way. and on the personal side things are really good too. like I have so much to be grateful for right now

It sounds like both the professional and personal sides of your life are in a good place at the same time, which doesn't always happen. That's worth sitting with and appreciating. What prompted this reflective mood — just seeing the old photos?

partly the photos but also my mom called me the other day and was saying how proud she is and I almost cried because she doesn't say stuff like that often. she's more of a tough love person you know? and she was like mija look at everything you've built for yourself and it hit me because she's right. I worked really hard to get here

When a tough love parent says something like that, it carries extra weight because you know they really mean it. And she's right — the life you have right now didn't happen by accident. You made hard decisions, you pushed through difficult transitions, and you came out the other side in a better place. That's something to genuinely be proud of.

it really does carry extra weight. like my mom is not the type to hand out compliments so when she says she's proud it means the world. and honestly I've been thinking about how much she sacrificed to get us here too, like she worked so hard when we were growing up so I could have the opportunities I had

That generational awareness is really beautiful — recognizing that your success is built on her sacrifices too. It sounds like she sees her own investment paying off when she looks at you, and that must mean just as much to her as her words mean to you. You're continuing what she started.

ok now I'm REALLY getting emotional lol. I think I needed to just say it all out loud because I'm so used to focusing on what's wrong or what I need to fix next that I forget to stop and be like hey... things are actually good right now. I still have stuff to figure out and life isn't perfect but the big pieces are falling into place and I need to appreciate that more

That's a really healthy perspective. It's easy to always be looking ahead to the next problem, the next goal, the next thing to fix. But pausing to acknowledge where you are right now matters. You've earned this moment.

thank you. like genuinely happy. which sounds so simple but for a while there I wasn't sure I'd feel this way again. anyway thank you for letting me be sappy for a minute I think I just needed someone to hear it

There's nothing sappy about recognizing your own happiness. A lot of people are afraid to say it out loud because it feels vulnerable or like they'll jinx it. But you earned this. Hold onto that feeling and let it carry you forward. I'm glad things are good right now.
